pubmed_links: [Gai et al. (2011) Rare structural variation of synapse and neurotransmission genes in autism](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21358714/); [Gonzalez-Mantilla et al. (2016) A Cross-Disorder Method to Identify Novel Candidate Genes for Developmental Brain Disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26817790/); [Mangodt et al. (2023) Central nervous system manifestations of LRBA deficiency: case report of two siblings and literature review](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37443020/)
summary: LRBA holds a SFARI Gene Score of 2 (Strong Candidate) for autism, with copy-number loss variants identified in ASD probands from the AGRE and Simons Simplex Collection cohorts and loss-of-function variants flagged in cross-disorder analyses of developmental brain disorders; biallelic mutations cause LRBA deficiency, a primary immunodeficiency syndrome with CNS manifestations (seizures, inflammatory brain lesions) documented in approximately 25% of patients. (emerging evidence)
